eval-001.rationale: Both show nearly identical views of mostly blue sky with a thin terrain strip at the bottom, neither achieving the expected close ground-level view of the Eiffel Tower.

eval-002.rationale: Both produce virtually identical top-down aerial views of central Paris with the Eiffel Tower area and Seine River clearly visible, matching the expected outcome equally well.

eval-003.rationale: A shows a clearer south-to-north perspective with the Seine in the foreground and Trocadero visible beyond, while B shifts to a more westward overhead angle that makes the Trocadero less prominent.

eval-004.rationale: Both show nearly identical oblique views of Midtown Manhattan from the east looking west with comparable framing and building detail.

eval-005.rationale: B renders 3D buildings prominently showing the Manhattan skyline from across the Hudson, while A shows a distant flat view where buildings are barely discernible.

eval-006.rationale: B delivers a dramatic close-up view into the Grand Canyon with layered rock formations and visible depth, while A shows a distant flat perspective where the canyon is barely perceptible.

eval-007.rationale: Both show comparable top-down aerial views of the Grand Canyon terrain at similar altitudes with only marginal differences in zoom level.

eval-008.rationale: B renders 3D skyscrapers looking south down Manhattan with prominent building detail, while A shows a flat high-altitude overhead view without 3D building relief.
